output:
M21 complete. Here's the test summary:

**Execute Terminal Injection Flow — Test Results (M21)**

| Test | Result |
|------|--------|
| `POST /api/terminal/MOAT/inject` with no session | 404 + helpful hint |
| `POST /api/terminal/MOAT/inject` with active session | 200 OK, prompt injected |
| `POST /api/northstar/MOAT/execute` with no session | `mode=work`, task files created (correct fallback) |
| `POST /api/northstar/MOAT/execute` with active session | `mode=terminal`, injected via PTY |
| Transient PTY error handling | 500 on first call (stale PTY), succeeds on retry; execute's `except: pass` fallback works |
| Bytes encoding | `proc.write((prompt + "\n").encode())` correct — ptyprocess needs bytes |

**Findings:** Flow works end-to-end. One side effect: when execute falls back to work mode, it creates task files — 3 duplicate M21 files were created during testing and have been cleaned up.
